items.title: Arrival (First 72 Hours) — Official
items.summary: Authoritative, non-optional onboarding procedures for Omani Ministry of Higher Education scholars upon physical arrival in the United States. This section governs border entry, SEVIS activation, institutional registration, and immediate post-arrival compliance.

scope.failure_impact: Visa status termination, SEVIS record invalidation, scholarship suspension

timeline.T0: Landing at U.S. port of entry

details: Carry passport, U.S. visa, and I-20 (F-1) or DS-2019 (J-1) in hand luggage, not checked baggage.
Documents must be presented to CBP officers upon request without delay.
Do not surrender original documents to third parties except authorized CBP officers or university officials.
mitigation: Immediate correction request if error is detected at entry

details: Confirm correct visa class (F-1 or J-1) is recorded upon entry.
Verify that the I-94 admission record lists 'D/S' (Duration of Status).
If admitted incorrectly, request correction immediately before leaving the inspection area.
post_entry_actions: Retrieve I-94 record online within 24 hours
Archive entry stamp and electronic record

details: Check-in with the Designated School Official is mandatory for SEVIS activation.
Check-in methods may include in-person appointment, online portal submission, or hybrid process.
Failure to check in may result in automatic SEVIS termination.
required_inputs: Copy of entry stamp and I-94

details: A valid U.S. residential address must be reported to the university and SEVIS.
Temporary housing (hotel, Airbnb) is acceptable initially but must be updated once permanent housing is secured.
Address changes must be reported within 10 days.

links: Authority: U.S. DHS SEVP (Study in the States)
Authority: Ministry of Higher Education scholarship guidance
Authority: U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP)

items.title: Immigration & Compliance — Official
items.summary: Binding immigration, employment, and status-maintenance obligations. No discretionary interpretation is permitted. When in doubt, escalation is mandatory.

details: Any form of employment, paid or unpaid, on-campus or off-campus, requires prior written authorization.
This includes internships, research assistantships, fellowships, startups, consulting, freelancing, and remote work.
Volunteer work is not exempt if it resembles a paid role.
authorization_sources: Ministry of Higher Education sponsor approval
common_violations: Online freelance work for non-U.S. entities

details: Passport must remain valid at least six months into the future unless exempt by treaty.
I-20 or DS-2019 end date must always reflect actual program duration.
Visa expiry affects re-entry, not lawful presence, but must still be tracked.
Changes in major, funding source, study level, or enrollment status must be approved in advance.
Unauthorized reduced course loads are a status violation.
Leave of absence requires coordinated DSO and sponsor approval.

links: Authority: U.S. DHS SEVP (Study in the States)
Authority: Ministry of Higher Education scholarship compliance

items.title: Academic & Living Setup — Official
items.summary: Operational setup requirements tied directly to legal status, scholarship continuity, and academic standing.

details: University ID issuance, email activation, and learning platform access are mandatory.
Orientation attendance may be legally required for international students.
Missed onboarding deadlines can delay enrollment verification.
Continuous health insurance coverage meeting university and sponsor standards is mandatory.
Waivers require formal approval and documented equivalency.
Coverage gaps may trigger registration holds or sponsor sanctions.
Minimum full-time enrollment must be maintained each term.
Grades, progress reports, and transcripts may be required by MoHE on a fixed cadence.
Academic probation must be reported immediately.

links: Authority: Ministry of Higher Education scholarship guidance

items.title: Escalation Rules — Official
items.summary: Non-negotiable escalation hierarchy for uncertainty, emergencies, or compliance ambiguity.

details: Do not rely on peer advice, online forums, or assumptions.
Escalate directly to the DSO and MoHE sponsor contact.
Written confirmation is required before acting.
Contact local emergency services (911) immediately.
Notify university emergency contacts after safety is secured.
Do not attempt self-diagnosis or peer medical guidance.
If official guidance is unclear or unavailable, explicitly pause action.
Document the question and request formal clarification.
Proceed only after receiving authoritative instruction.
